name: Cow milk, pasteurized, vitamin A + D added, 1% fat (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (milk density ~1.03 g/mL; concentration_mM computed per liter of milk)

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA SR Legacy 01082 (total sugars 5.0 g/100g, essentially all lactose); milkfacts.info lactose 5.26 g/100g
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source of milk; a disaccharide of glucose+galactose. Total sugars in USDA panel are essentially all lactose.

components.name: D-galactose
components.source: Milk free-sugar literature (free galactose trace); FooDB FOOD00890
components.evidence: Free monosaccharide present only in trace; the bulk of galactose is bound in lactose. Value is an order-of-magnitude estimate; typically <20 mg/100g.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: Milk free-sugar literature (free glucose 0-14 mg/100g); FooDB FOOD00890
components.evidence: Free glucose present only in trace; bulk is bound in lactose. Order-of-magnitude estimate.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: White & Davies / dairy chemistry literature; ResearchGate 304025254 'Variability of citric acid in cow milk' (mean ~0.139-0.147%); textbook ~1.75 g/L
components.evidence: MOST ABUNDANT organic acid in milk (~1.5-1.8 g/L). Key metabolite; was MISSING from the FooDB profile. Range 0.14-0.18 g/100g, midpoint used.

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Dairy chemistry literature (fresh pasteurized milk L-lactate very low, <10 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Very low in fresh pasteurized milk; rises with bacterial fermentation/age. Present as L-lactate (already in FooDB list). Value approximate.

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: AOAC J. 87(1):116, 2004 (PMID 15084094) — orotic acid 30-70 ug/mL in mature bovine milk
components.evidence: Characteristic pyrimidine-pathway metabolite of milk (30-70 mg/L). Midpoint ~50 mg/L = 5 mg/100g.

components.name: uric acid
components.source: AOAC J. 87(1):116, 2004 (PMID 15084094) — uric acid 9-24 ug/mL in bovine milk
components.evidence: Minor purine catabolite, 9-24 mg/L; midpoint used.

components.name: pyruvate
components.evidence: Minor organic acid present in milk; typically <5 mg/100g, unquantified here.

components.name: urea
components.source: Milk urea nitrogen (MUN) literature; typical MUN ~10-14 mg/dL N -> urea ~21-30 mg/100g
components.evidence: Largest non-protein-nitrogen fraction of milk; usable N source. Midpoint ~25 mg/100g.

components.name: creatine
components.evidence: Minor non-protein-N metabolite; creatinine measured at 6-12 mg/L (PMID 15084094). Present, low.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Casein (~80%) + whey (~20%); the amino-acid profile below is the protein-derived total.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Total glutamic acid content; most abundant amino acid in milk protein.

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Total amino acid content; high in casein.

components.name: calcium
components.evidence: Largely bound to casein micelles / phosphate.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Major anion; not in USDA SR panel. Approximate typical value.

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: Sulfur mostly in protein; free sulfate minor, unquantified.

components.name: iodine
components.source: Dairy iodine literature (cow milk ~15-30 ug/100g; not in USDA SR panel)
components.evidence: Milk is a major dietary iodine source; approximate typical value, seasonally variable.

components.name: cobalt
components.evidence: Ultratrace; component of cobalamin (B12). Present, unquantified.

components.name: riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Milk is a leading dietary source of riboflavin.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.source: USDA SR Legacy 01082 (food folate 5 ug; DFE 2 ug)

components.name: vitamin B12 (cobalamin)
components.evidence: Milk is a significant B12 source (~25% DV per 100g).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Trace pro-vitamin A carotenoid from milkfat; low in 1% milk.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: 1% milkfat; triacylglycerols dominate, plus milk-fat-globule-membrane phospholipids.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1, MUFA)
components.source: USDA SR Legacy 01082 (total MUFA 0.21, predominantly oleic)

components.name: short/medium-chain fatty acids (butyric C4, caproic C6, caprylic C8, capric C10, lauric C12)
components.evidence: Characteristic milk-fat short/medium-chain acids (hexanoic, octanoic, decanoic, dodecanoic in FooDB list). Collectively ~10-12% of milk fat; individually small at 1% fat.

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: Genuine animal sterol of milk (KEPT — milk is an animal food; not implausible here).

components.name: phosphatidylcholines and sphingomyelin (milk-fat-globule-membrane)
components.evidence: Genuine membrane phospholipids of the milk fat globule; individually minor. Kept (no BiGG mets -> uncovered).

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic trace heavy metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-essential ultratrace element, not a growth nutrient (analogous to strontium/barium)

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: non-essential ultratrace element for microbial growth; FooDB noise

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: non-essential ultratrace contaminant at milk levels; not a meaningful growth nutrient

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-essential ultratrace element; FooDB noise, not a growth nutrient here

remove.name: D-Galactarate
remove.reason: mucic acid; implausible/predicted, not an established milk metabolite (FooDB noise)

remove.name: Acetone
remove.reason: trace volatile ketone-body carryover, not a growth-relevant nutrient

remove.name: Methanol
remove.reason: trace volatile, not a growth-relevant nutrient of milk

remove.name: CO2 / O2 / H2O placeholders (CO2, Co2+ duplicate, H+, O2)
remove.reason: dissolved-gas / proton / ion placeholders, not compositional nutrients

missing_added: citric acid (~0.16 g/100g — dominant organic acid, was absent)
lactose amount (~5.0 g/100g — dominant carbon source, quantified)
full quantitative amino-acid, vitamin, mineral and fatty-acid panel from USDA SR Legacy 01082
keep_uncovered: phosphatidylcholines PC(16:0/18:1) and other milk-fat-globule-membrane PCs (genuine, no BiGG met)
glycine betaine, taurine, myo-inositol, carnitine/acylcarnitines, creatine/creatinine, kynurenine (genuine minor milk metabolites)
sources: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 01082 (Milk, lowfat, fluid, 1% milkfat, with added vitamin A and vitamin D) — full nutrient/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel
milkfacts.info Nutrient Content of Milk Varieties (lactose, mineral cross-check)
ResearchGate 304025254 — Variability of citric acid in cow milk composition (citrate ~0.14-0.15%)
AOAC J. 87(1):116, 2004 / PMID 15084094 — orotic acid 30-70 ug/mL, uric acid 9-24 ug/mL, creatinine 6-12 ug/mL in bovine milk
Milk urea nitrogen (MUN) dairy literature — urea ~21-30 mg/100g
Dairy iodine and chloride composition literature
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is LACTOSE (~5 g/100g, ~146 mM); citrate (~0.16 g/100g) is the principal organic acid and was missing from the FooDB profile, with orotic/uric acids and quantified urea also added. Fixed-composition (non-plant, cow-milk 1% fat) medium: rich in casein/whey amino acids, calcium/phosphorus, B-vitamins (esp. riboflavin, B12) and fortified vitamins A+D; iron-poor. Cholesterol is KEPT (genuine animal sterol, unlike the plant-food case); removals are trace heavy metals/contaminants and gas/ion placeholders.
